class.name: IRCDCCTransfer
class.brief_description: Represents an active or pending Direct Client-to-Client file transfer.
class.description: Handles the status, metrics, tracking, and port mapping logic required to initiate or receive an outgoing DCC SEND block over the networking protocol layer safely utilizing asynchronous connections.

method.name: accept
method.description: Confirms an inbound request connection asynchronously by initializing an available receiver socket and commencing the binary byte stream buffer processing immediately.

method.name: cancel
method.description: Aborts an active byte transmission connection natively, closing underlying stream peers and socket structures to prevent further buffer leaking.

method.name: get_progress
method.description: Returns the calculated progression of the byte payload transition over the active stream, expressed as a generalized percentage.

method.name: set_resume_position
method.description: Sets the resume position. [param position] - Position.

members.member: Remote IP address used for the DCC connection.
Total size of the file being transferred in bytes.
Name of the file being sent or received.
Local filesystem path where the file will be saved or read from.
Port used for the DCC connection.
Nickname of the remote IRC user involved in the transfer.
Number of bytes transferred so far.
Whether to use IPv6 for the connection.

constants.constant: Represents an outgoing file transfer (sending a file to a remote user).
Represents an incoming file transfer (receiving a file from a remote user).
Represents a DCC chat session (direct peer-to-peer messaging).
The transfer has been created but not yet started.
The transfer is attempting to establish a connection.
The transfer is actively sending or receiving data.
The transfer encountered an error and failed.
The transfer was cancelled before completion.
